lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A review Go team handles case 1398 during a publisher-fairness incident rehearsal. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile Go, pgx, migration, dependency, clean-build, and artifact evidence; every goroutine, body, row set, transaction, lease, timer, and pool needs named cleanup ownership; then change one RSS, Atom, namespace, date, identity, content, or encoding case and reconcile provenance. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Reproduce format, generated artifacts, migration validation, unit, integration, race, fuzz, vet, vulnerability, build, and smoke evidence from clean state.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: A cached developer build is reproducible release evidence.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Reproduce format, generated artifacts, migration validation, unit, integration, race, fuzz, vet, vulnerability, build, and smoke evidence from clean state. Artifact under review: reproducible Go feed-service repository baseline.

options.text: For separate evidence from confidence, import one convenient trusted RSS feed, omit reader and publisher outcomes, revision, discovery and SSRF scope, Atom and namespace cases, XML and HTTP budgets, PostgreSQL constraints and transaction, schedule, lease, goroutine cleanup, tenant, accessibility, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as feed-service proof. This does not satisfy Feed Go Clean Gates for reproducible Go feed-service repository baseline.
The record names the assumption, applies Feed Go Clean Gates, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Feed Go Clean Gates record says reproducible Go feed-service repository baseline looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary for Feed Go Clean Gates.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Record what required effort · Review Feed Go Clean Gates
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Feed Go Clean Gates: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A review Go team handles case 7697 during a multilingual university archive import. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile Go, pgx, migration, dependency, clean-build, and artifact evidence; browser Go must remain deterministic and free of network, database, process, and host effects; then inject one URL, DNS, redirect, compression, entity, depth, timeout, or publisher-budget failure and reject it safely. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Reproduce format, generated artifacts, migration validation, unit, integration, race, fuzz, vet, vulnerability, build, and smoke evidence from clean state.

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and Go Record what required effort · Review Feed Go Clean Gates.
steps.why: Retrieving Feed Go Clean Gates through the concrete reproducible Go feed-service repository baseline case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Feed aggregation and Go Record what required effort · Review Feed Go Clean Gates, keep Feed Go Clean Gates connected to executable Go feed, XML, HTTP, PostgreSQL, schedule, API, reader, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Reproduce format, generated artifacts, migration validation, unit, integration, race, fuzz, vet, vulnerability, build, and smoke evidence from clean state.

Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only.
